headline: Malaysia Green Building Index Adoption Reaches 12% Only
context: Only 12% of new commercial and residential buildings in Malaysia achieved Green Building Index certification in 2025.

cards.big: Only 12% of new buildings achieved Green Building Index certification
cards.sub: Voluntary nature of GBI limits adoption as developers avoid additional costs

cards.big: GBI-certified buildings cost 5-8% more to construct but save 30% on energy
cards.sub: Payback period of 4-6 years deters developers focused on sale price

cards.big: Singapore mandates Green Mark for all new buildings achieving 82% green rate
cards.sub: Malaysia lacks mandatory green building requirement for any building category

cards.big: Tax incentives for green buildings attracted only 280 applications in 2025
cards.sub: Maximum RM5 million deduction covers small fraction of typical project cost

cards.big: Green building adoption tests whether sustainability can advance through incentives alone
12% green building adoption. Market incentives and code enforcement must work together, not in isolation.
